reasoning: Bobby Finke set the 1500m freestyle world record at the Paris 2024 Olympics (14:30.67). Evidence from the Wikipedia page as of November 4, 2025, confirms he still holds the record, having survived the 2025 World Aquatics Championships in Singapore (July/August 2025). The 1500m freestyle world record is historically very durable, often standing for several years or even over a decade (e.g., Grant Hackett's stood for 10 years, Sun Yang's for 12). While there are some long-course meets in the forecast window (Chinese National Games in Nov 2025 and U.S. Open in Dec 2025), the 1500m is rarely broken outside of peak championship season (Summer). Daniel Wiffen and other top competitors are likely focusing on the short-course season during the winter months. Therefore, the probability of the record being broken before February 2026 is extremely low.
